memberdef.argsstring: (psa_storage_uid_t uid, size_t data_length, const void *p_data, psa_storage_create_flags_t create_flags)
memberdef.name: psa_ps_set

briefdescription.para: Creates a new or modifies an existing entry.

parameterdescription.para: The identifier of the data. Must be nonzero.
The size in bytes of the data in p_data to store.
A buffer containing the data to store.
Flags indicating the properties of the entry.
The operation completed successfully.
An unspecified internal failure happened.
An entry associated with the provided uid already exists and was created with PSA_STORAGE_FLAG_WRITE_ONCE.
One or more of the flags provided in create_flags are not supported or not valid.
One or more arguments other than create_flags are invalid.
There is insufficient space on the storage medium.
The physical storage has failed (fatal error).

memberdef.argsstring: (psa_storage_uid_t uid, size_t data_offset, size_t data_size, void *p_data, size_t *p_data_length)
memberdef.name: psa_ps_get

briefdescription.para: Retrieves data associated with the provided uid.

parameterdescription.para: The identifier of the data.
The offset, in bytes, from which to start reading the data.
The number of bytes to read.
The buffer where the data will be placed on success. Must be at least data_size bytes long.
On success, the number of bytes placed in p_data.
The operation completed successfully.
An unspecified internal failure happened.
One or more arguments are invalid. This can also happen if data_offset is larger than the size of the data associated with uid.
The provided uid was not found in the storage.
The physical storage has failed (fatal error).
The data associated with uid failed authentication.
The data associated with uid is corrupt.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the metadata of a given entry.

parameterdescription.para: The identifier of the entry.
A pointer to a psa_storage_info_t struct that will be populated with the metadata on success.
The operation completed successfully.
An unspecified internal failure happened.
uid is invalid.
The provided uid was not found in the storage.
The physical storage has failed (fatal error).
The data associated with uid failed authentication.
The data associated with uid is corrupt.

briefdescription.para: Removes the provided uid and its associated data.

detaileddescription.para: Deletes previously stored data and any associated metadata, including rollback protection data.

parameterdescription.para: The identifier of the entry to remove.

simplesect.para: A status indicating the success/failure of the operation

parameterdescription.para: The operation completed successfully.
An unspecified internal failure happened.
The entry was created with PSA_STORAGE_FLAG_WRITE_ONCE.
uid is invalid.
The provided uid was not found in the storage.
The physical storage has failed (fatal error).

briefdescription.para: Reserves storage for the provided uid.

detaileddescription.para: Upon success, the capacity of the storage for uid will be capacity, and the size will be 0. It is only necessary to call this function for data that will be written with the psa_ps_set_extended() function. If only the psa_ps_set() function is used, calls to this function are redundant. This function cannot be used to replace or resize an existing entry.

parameterdescription.para: The identifier of the entry to reserve storage for.
The capacity, in bytes, to allocate.
Flags indicating the properties of the entry.
The operation completed successfully.
An unspecified internal failure happened.
The implementation doesn't support this function or one or more of the flags provided in create_flags are not supported or not valid.
uid is invalid.
An entry with the provided uid already exists.
There is insufficient space on the storage medium.
The physical storage has failed (fatal error).

memberdef.argsstring: (psa_storage_uid_t uid, size_t data_offset, size_t data_length, const void *p_data)
memberdef.name: psa_ps_set_extended

briefdescription.para: Writes part of the data associated with the provided uid.

detaileddescription.para: Before calling this function, storage must have been reserved with a call to psa_ps_create(). This function can also be used to overwrite data that was written with psa_ps_set(). This function can overwrite existing data and/or extend it up to the capacity of the entry specified in psa_ps_create(), but cannot create gaps.

parameterdescription.para: The identifier of the entry to write.
The offset, in bytes, from which to start writing the data. Can be at most the current size of the data.

The size in bytes of the data in p_data to write. data_offset
data_length can be at most the capacity of the entry.
A buffer containing the data to write.
The operation completed successfully.
An unspecified internal failure happened.
The entry was created with PSA_STORAGE_FLAG_WRITE_ONCE.
The implementation doesn't support this function.
One or more arguments are invalid.
The provided uid was not found in the storage.
The physical storage has failed (fatal error).
The data associated with uid failed authentication.
The data associated with uid is corrupt.

simplesect.para: A bitmask with flags set for the optional features supported by the implementation. Currently defined flags are limited to PSA_STORAGE_SUPPORT_SET_EXTENDED.

detaileddescription.para: For more information on the PS, see The Protected Storage API.
